    19‚Üí- [x] Bibliography entries reference [ErRe66], [Po76], [Ko77], [KoSz83].
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üí## 4. Namespace

Consider adding simple tests (e.g., verifying `edgeCount` on a small graph) to confirm the definitions behave as expected. This is optional per the checklist ("consider adding").

    51‚Üí  The source at [erdosproblems.com/746](https://www.erdosproblems.com/746) asks:
    52‚Üí

# Review: `deepmind/746.lean` ‚Äî Erd≈ës Problem 746

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd≈ës Problem 746`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/746`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (Erd≈ës‚ÄìR√©nyi conjecture on Hamiltonicity of random graphs).
- [x] Bibliography entries reference [ErRe66], [Po76], [Ko77], [KoSz83].

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos746 ... end Erdos746`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos746`.
- [x] `open SimpleGraph Finset` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `toGraph` has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `edgeCount` has a docstring.
- [x] `graphsWithEdges` has a docstring.
- [x] `gnmFraction` has a docstring.
- [x] `gnmFraction` is marked `noncomputable` (it uses real-valued division). The other three definitions (`toGraph`, `edgeCount`, `graphsWithEdges`) operate on decidable `Bool`/`Finset` types and do not require `noncomputable`.
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] Function names use `lowerCamelCase`: `toGraph`, `edgeCount`, `graphsWithEdges`, `gnmFraction`.
- [x] No special notation needed; no `local notation` required.
- [ ] **(Suggestion)** No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity-check declarations for the custom definitions. Consider adding simple tests (e.g., verifying `edgeCount` on a small graph) to confirm the definitions behave as expected. This is optional per the checklist ("consider adding").

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_746` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5 60]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[ErRe66, KoSz83]`.

### `answer()` usage

- [ ] **ISSUE ‚Äî The original problem is phrased as a yes/no question, but no `answer()` wrapper is used.**

  The source at [erdosproblems.com/746](https://www.erdosproblems.com/746) asks:

  > *"Is it true that, almost surely, a random graph on n vertices with ‚â• (1/2+Œµ)n log n edges is Hamiltonian?"*

  Per the checklist ¬ß6 ("How to decide which case applies"), "is it true that" triggers **Case 1**. Since the problem has been proved affirmatively, the theorem should use:

  ```lean
  theorem erdos_746 : answer(True) ‚Üî
      ‚àÄ Œµ : ‚Ñù, Œµ > 0 ‚Üí
      ‚àÄ Œ¥ : ‚Ñù, Œ¥ > 0 ‚Üí
      ‚àÉ n‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• n‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
        ‚àÄ m : ‚Ñï, (m : ‚Ñù) ‚â• (1/2 + Œµ) * (n : ‚Ñù) * Real.log (n : ‚Ñù) ‚Üí
          m ‚â§ n.choose 2 ‚Üí
          gnmFraction n m (fun G => G.IsHamiltonian) ‚â• 1 - Œ¥ := by
    sorry
  ```

  The current formulation uses Case 3 (direct assertion without `answer()`), which is appropriate when the problem says "Show that..." or "Prove that..." but not when it asks "Is it true that...?"

- [x] Quantifiers are inside the theorem type (not as parameters). No `answer()` parameters to check, but if `answer(True)` is added, quantifiers would correctly remain on the RHS.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` ‚Äî valid and appropriate (proved by Koml√≥s and Szemer√©di, but no formal Lean proof).
- [x] Not `research open`, so the sorry-free proof check is N/A.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 60`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5, 60).
- [x] Numbers are in ascending order: 5 < 60.
- [x] No duplicates.
- [x] Both codes are valid AMS MSC2020 codes: **5** (combinatorics), **60** (probability theory and stochastic processes).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Proof uses the multi-line `by\n  sorry` form, which is acceptable per the checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declaration.
- [x] No informal proof sketches (appropriate for `research solved`).
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A ‚Äî No variants present. The file formalizes the single main conjecture.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚àÄ`, `‚àÉ`, `‚â•`, `‚âÝ`, `‚àß`, `‚Üí`, `‚Ñù`, `‚Ñï`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] **Not verified** ‚Äî `lake build` was not run. Must be confirmed before merge.
- [ ] **Not verified** ‚Äî Custom linter passes not confirmed.

---

## Summary

**Overall: GOOD with one substantive issue.**

The file is clean, well-documented, and follows nearly all conventions. The four custom definitions (`toGraph`, `edgeCount`, `graphsWithEdges`, `gnmFraction`) are well-structured with docstrings and appropriate use of `noncomputable`. The theorem statement, namespace, attributes, and code quality all conform.

**One issue to resolve before approval:**

1. **`answer()` wrapper missing (¬ß6, Case 1):** The original problem is phrased "Is it true that...?" which requires `answer(True) ‚Üî P` form per the checklist. The current direct-assertion form (Case 3) should be updated.

**Minor suggestion (non-blocking):**

- Consider adding `@[category test]` sanity checks for the custom definitions (¬ß5).

    11‚Üídistributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12‚ÜíWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 746
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/746](https://www.erdosproblems.com/746)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA conjecture of Erd≈ës and R√©nyi [ErRe66]. P√≥sa [Po76] showed this with $Cn \log n$ edges
    25‚Üífor some large $C$, Korshunov [Ko77] improved the threshold, and Koml√≥s and Szemer√©di [KoSz83]
    26‚Üíproved the sharp result that with $(1/2)n \log n + (1/2)n \log \log n + cn$ edges the probability
    27‚Üíof being Hamiltonian tends to $e^{-e^{-2c}}$.
    28‚Üí-/
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Finset
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üínamespace Erdos746
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí/-- The simple graph on $\operatorname{Fin} n$ determined by a Boolean edge configuration. -/
    35‚Üídef toGraph {n : ‚Ñï} (ec : Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Bool) : SimpleGraph (Fin n) where
    36‚Üí  Adj u v := u ‚âÝ v ‚àß ec (min u v) (max u v) = true
    37‚Üí  symm := fun _ _ ‚ü®hne, h‚ü© => ‚ü®hne.symm, by rwa [min_comm, max_comm]‚ü©
    38‚Üí  loopless := ‚ü®fun v ‚ü®h, _‚ü© => h rfl‚ü©
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- The number of edges in a Boolean edge configuration on $\operatorname{Fin} n$
    41‚Üí(counting only pairs $i < j$ to avoid double-counting). -/
    42‚Üídef edgeCount {n : ‚Ñï} (ec : Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Bool) : ‚Ñï :=
    43‚Üí  (Finset.univ.filter (fun p : Fin n √ó Fin n => p.1 < p.2 ‚àß ec p.1 p.2 = true)).card
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- The set of all edge configurations on $\operatorname{Fin} n$ with exactly $m$ edges. -/
    46‚Üídef graphsWithEdges (n m : ‚Ñï) : Finset (Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Bool) :=
    47‚Üí  Finset.univ.filter (fun ec => edgeCount ec = m)
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- The fraction of graphs on $\operatorname{Fin} n$ with exactly $m$ edges that satisfy
    50‚Üíproperty $P$ (the $G(n,m)$ probability of $P$). Returns $0$ if there are
    51‚Üíno graphs with exactly $m$ edges. -/
    52‚Üínoncomputable def gnmFraction (n m : ‚Ñï) (P : SimpleGraph (Fin n) ‚Üí Prop) : ‚Ñù :=
    53‚Üí  ((graphsWithEdges n m).filter (fun ec => P (toGraph ec))).card /
    54‚Üí  ((graphsWithEdges n m).card : ‚Ñù)
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/--
    57‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 746 [ErRe66, KoSz83]:
    58‚Üí
    59‚ÜíFor every $\varepsilon > 0$, almost surely a random graph on $n$ vertices with at least
    60‚Üí$(1/2 + \varepsilon) \cdot n \cdot \log n$ edges is Hamiltonian.
    61‚Üí
    62‚ÜíFormally: for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and $\delta > 0$, there exists $n_0$ such that for all
    63‚Üí$n \geq n_0$ and all $m$ with $(1/2 + \varepsilon) \cdot n \cdot \log n \leq m \leq \binom{n}{2}$,
    64‚Üíthe $G(n,m)$-probability that the graph is Hamiltonian is at least $1 - \delta$.
    65‚Üí
    66‚ÜíProved by Koml√≥s and Szemer√©di [KoSz83].
    67‚Üí-/
    68‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 60]
    69‚Üítheorem erdos_746 :
    70‚Üí    ‚àÄ Œµ : ‚Ñù, Œµ > 0 ‚Üí
    71‚Üí    ‚àÄ Œ¥ : ‚Ñù, Œ¥ > 0 ‚Üí
    72‚Üí    ‚àÉ n‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• n‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
    73‚Üí      ‚àÄ m : ‚Ñï, (m : ‚Ñù) ‚â• (1/2 + Œµ) * (n : ‚Ñù) * Real.log (n : ‚Ñù) ‚Üí
    74‚Üí        m ‚â§ n.choose 2 ‚Üí
    75‚Üí        gnmFraction n m (fun G => G.IsHamiltonian) ‚â• 1 - Œ¥ := by
    76‚Üí  sorry
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üíend Erdos746
    79‚Üí

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 746

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/746](https://www.erdosproblems.com/746)

A conjecture of Erd≈ës and R√©nyi [ErRe66]. P√≥sa [Po76] showed this with $Cn \log n$ edges
for some large $C$, Korshunov [Ko77] improved the threshold, and Koml√≥s and Szemer√©di [KoSz83]
proved the sharp result that with $(1/2)n \log n + (1/2)n \log \log n + cn$ edges the probability
of being Hamiltonian tends to $e^{-e^{-2c}}$.
-/

open SimpleGraph Finset

namespace Erdos746

/-- The simple graph on $\operatorname{Fin} n$ determined by a Boolean edge configuration. -/
def toGraph {n : ‚Ñï} (ec : Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Bool) : SimpleGraph (Fin n) where
  Adj u v := u ‚âÝ v ‚àß ec (min u v) (max u v) = true
  symm := fun _ _ ‚ü®hne, h‚ü© => ‚ü®hne.symm, by rwa [min_comm, max_comm]‚ü©
  loopless := ‚ü®fun v ‚ü®h, _‚ü© => h rfl‚ü©

/-- The number of edges in a Boolean edge configuration on $\operatorname{Fin} n$
(counting only pairs $i < j$ to avoid double-counting). -/
def edgeCount {n : ‚Ñï} (ec : Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Bool) : ‚Ñï :=
  (Finset.univ.filter (fun p : Fin n √ó Fin n => p.1 < p.2 ‚àß ec p.1 p.2 = true)).card

/-- The set of all edge configurations on $\operatorname{Fin} n$ with exactly $m$ edges. -/
def graphsWithEdges (n m : ‚Ñï) : Finset (Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Bool) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun ec => edgeCount ec = m)

/-- The fraction of graphs on $\operatorname{Fin} n$ with exactly $m$ edges that satisfy
property $P$ (the $G(n,m)$ probability of $P$). Returns $0$ if there are
no graphs with exactly $m$ edges. -/
noncomputable def gnmFraction (n m : ‚Ñï) (P : SimpleGraph (Fin n) ‚Üí Prop) : ‚Ñù :=
  ((graphsWithEdges n m).filter (fun ec => P (toGraph ec))).card /
  ((graphsWithEdges n m).card : ‚Ñù)

/--
Erd≈ës Problem 746 [ErRe66, KoSz83]:

For every $\varepsilon > 0$, almost surely a random graph on $n$ vertices with at least
$(1/2 + \varepsilon) \cdot n \cdot \log n$ edges is Hamiltonian.

Formally: for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and $\delta > 0$, there exists $n_0$ such that for all
$n \geq n_0$ and all $m$ with $(1/2 + \varepsilon) \cdot n \cdot \log n \leq m \leq \binom{n}{2}$,
the $G(n,m)$-probability that the graph is Hamiltonian is at least $1 - \delta$.

Proved by Koml√≥s and Szemer√©di [KoSz83].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5 60]
theorem erdos_746 :
    ‚àÄ Œµ : ‚Ñù, Œµ > 0 ‚Üí
    ‚àÄ Œ¥ : ‚Ñù, Œ¥ > 0 ‚Üí
    ‚àÉ n‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• n‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
      ‚àÄ m : ‚Ñï, (m : ‚Ñù) ‚â• (1/2 + Œµ) * (n : ‚Ñù) * Real.log (n : ‚Ñù) ‚Üí
        m ‚â§ n.choose 2 ‚Üí
        gnmFraction n m (fun G => G.IsHamiltonian) ‚â• 1 - Œ¥ := by
  sorry

end Erdos746

134:**How to decide which case applies:** Read the problem's English description (module docstring, theorem docstring, or source reference). If it asks a yes/no question ‚Äî "asked whether", "is it true that", "does there exist", "can one" ‚Äî use Case 1. If it asks for a specific value ‚Äî "what is", "find the minimum", "determine" ‚Äî use Case 2. If it asserts a fact ‚Äî "prove that", "show that", a direct inequality or bound ‚Äî use Case 3.

139-  - Linter `linter.style.category_attribute` enforces this.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/-

you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 746

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/746](https://www.erdosproblems.com/746)

A conjecture of Erd≈ës and R√©nyi [ErRe66]. P√≥sa [Po76] showed this with $Cn \log n$ edges
for some large $C$, Korshunov [Ko77] improved the threshold, and Koml√≥s and Szemer√©di [KoSz83]
proved the sharp result that with $(1/2)n \log n + (1/2)n \log \log n + cn$ edges the probability
of being Hamiltonian tends to $e^{-e^{-2c}}$.
-/

open SimpleGraph Finset

namespace Erdos746

/-- The simple graph on $\operatorname{Fin} n$ determined by a Boolean edge configuration. -/
def toGraph {n : ‚Ñï} (ec : Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Bool) : SimpleGraph (Fin n) where
  Adj u v := u ‚âÝ v ‚àß ec (min u v) (max u v) = true
  symm := fun _ _ ‚ü®hne, h‚ü© => ‚ü®hne.symm, by rwa [min_comm, max_comm]‚ü©
  loopless := ‚ü®fun v ‚ü®h, _‚ü© => h rfl‚ü©

/-- The number of edges in a Boolean edge configuration on $\operatorname{Fin} n$
(counting only pairs $i < j$ to avoid double-counting). -/
def edgeCount {n : ‚Ñï} (ec : Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Bool) : ‚Ñï :=
  (Finset.univ.filter (fun p : Fin n √ó Fin n => p.1 < p.2 ‚àß ec p.1 p.2 = true)).card

/-- The set of all edge configurations on $\operatorname{Fin} n$ with exactly $m$ edges. -/
def graphsWithEdges (n m : ‚Ñï) : Finset (Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Bool) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun ec => edgeCount ec = m)

/-- The fraction of graphs on $\operatorname{Fin} n$ with exactly $m$ edges that satisfy
property $P$ (the $G(n,m)$ probability of $P$). Returns $0$ if there are
no graphs with exactly $m$ edges. -/
noncomputable def gnmFraction (n m : ‚Ñï) (P : SimpleGraph (Fin n) ‚Üí Prop) : ‚Ñù :=
  ((graphsWithEdges n m).filter (fun ec => P (toGraph ec))).card /
  ((graphsWithEdges n m).card : ‚Ñù)

/--
Erd≈ës Problem 746 [ErRe66, KoSz83]:

For every $\varepsilon > 0$, almost surely a random graph on $n$ vertices with at least
$(1/2 + \varepsilon) \cdot n \cdot \log n$ edges is Hamiltonian.

Formally: for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and $\delta > 0$, there exists $n_0$ such that for all
$n \geq n_0$ and all $m$ with $(1/2 + \varepsilon) \cdot n \cdot \log n \leq m \leq \binom{n}{2}$,
the $G(n,m)$-probability that the graph is Hamiltonian is at least $1 - \delta$.

Proved by Koml√≥s and Szemer√©di [KoSz83].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5 60]
theorem erdos_746 :
    ‚àÄ Œµ : ‚Ñù, Œµ > 0 ‚Üí
    ‚àÄ Œ¥ : ‚Ñù, Œ¥ > 0 ‚Üí
    ‚àÉ n‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• n‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
      ‚àÄ m : ‚Ñï, (m : ‚Ñù) ‚â• (1/2 + Œµ) * (n : ‚Ñù) * Real.log (n : ‚Ñù) ‚Üí
        m ‚â§ n.choose 2 ‚Üí
        gnmFraction n m (fun G => G.IsHamiltonian) ‚â• 1 - Œ¥ := by
  sorry

end Erdos746
